title: Monotone physics-constrained rank stabilizer
summary: F1 tyre degradation is physically structured, but flexible ensembles can learn fold-specific reversals around tyre age, degradation, and finish-window margins.
rationale: F1 tyre degradation is physically structured, but flexible ensembles can learn fold-specific reversals around tyre age, degradation, and finish-window margins. XGBoost and LightGBM support monotonic constraints that can enforce broad physical trends while leaving categorical/race interactions unconstrained in the main model.
implementation_hint: Train a small XGBoost or LightGBM model on robust numeric features with constraints such as positive for TyreLife, Deg_Per_TyreLife, CurrentTyreFinishPressure, LapsBeyondFinishWindow and negative for CurrentTyreFinishMargin or LapsRemaining_Est where appropriate. Rank-average this constrained model with the unconstrained best model rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: May improve public leaderboard robustness by reducing overfit local reversals while preserving AUC-relevant ordering in high-confidence pit-window regions.
risk: Pit strategy is not globally monotone because safety cars, wet weather, and overcuts can invert relationships; constraints should be limited to a sidecar model with low ensemble weight.
